summary: Iteration-2 re-review of the Define-phase artifacts (requirements.md R1-R50, discovery_notes.md, open_questions.md) against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. The material change since iteration 1 is the newly-added human-in-the-loop modal section (R36-R50), confirmed by open_questions.md as new this gate; the three-panel requirements and the real-time tension were already passed. New content is guide-aligned: R37's overlay matches the UI guide's 'elevation is for menus/modals only'; R48 (focus moves into the modal on open, keyboard-closable, focus returns on close) matches the a11y baseline (keyboard-reachable, visible focus, semantic HTML); R49 mandates shared styling/spacing/components, matching 'one source of truth per component'; R45/R46/R47's negative paths impose no guide-conflicting behavior. Backend-wise, open_questions Q11-Q14 keep this a UI-only change routed through the existing Phases::AnswerQuestions service (business logic stays in the reusable PORO, controller thin, the state transition carries its rules in the service) -- consistent with the backend guide. The three panels remain aligned as in iteration 1 (aggregate reads to app/queries/, HomeController#index thin, broadcasts from services after commit, reuse of StatusHelper#status_badge and the prescribed card/badge/empty-state/type-scale conventions, status never by color alone per R12/R35). No current guide deviation exists in the spec: every requirement is implementable in a fully guide-compliant way. One watch-item for Plan/Build, not a spec defect: R40/R42 ('default appears as the input box's default text, shown in place until the user types' and is 'replaced' when they type) reads toward an HTML placeholder that doubles as the submitted value (R41). A literal placeholder-as-value implementation would risk the a11y baseline ('Color contrast >= 4.5:1 for text' -- placeholder text renders muted) and accessible-field semantics; the guide's Forms convention favors real field content with help text below. The requirements also admit the accessible reading (a real pre-filled, editable default value) which satisfies R40-R43 without the placeholder risk -- Plan should pick that mechanism. Because the spec permits a compliant implementation, this is recorded as guidance rather than a blocking finding.
